The first one is, well, you are at the mercy of a vendor or an API or a database changing.
So, if it does, well, now you need to go fix the connector to get your product to work.
The second one is, there are a lot of limitations around APIs on how much data you can collect, how much data you can have access to.

And when you have one user, it's fine, like you can get by, but the moment you start getting 10, 20, 30 users on your product, then all your connectors start breaking because these vendors have rate limits.
is, it's

in a way, connectors are a living organism, and if you start creating this living organism yourself, well, now you need to grow them yourself.

And context is more of the input.
If the outcome is good, you can assume that the context was good, but I don't think we have enough context to know about it yet.
I mean, if you're writing an article, and you provide context to that article, if this article does poorly, you're going to assume that the context you provided was bad.
But if the article does really well, then you're going to assume that the context you provided was bad.
So you guys have built Airbyte on open source principles.

If, if we look at, it isn't, it is a completely new, um, It's in a way it's a completely new industry that is being created.
And if you look at a lot of very successful company in the AI world, on infrastructure, almost all of them have something that is open source available.
Even, even open AI released their weight in open source and there, to me, there are two reasons for that.
The first one is the innovation is going so fast that it's very hard for just one.
And there is so much discovery and so much learning that this will happen.
That is very hard for one company to just know what the

And instead, what all these companies are doing is actually casting a very wide net to have as many people using their products, we can talk about Langchain, can talk about all these companies, and try to understand what are the patterns that are emerging, which is very hard to do if you keep everything close to you.

And same thing for models, like, you know, when the first LLM started, people just used it to do like, like, everyone who started to code with an LLM started with a very dumb hello world, and then people started to discover what it does, how far you can push it, and this is only possible if you start open sourcing a lot of these technologies.
And then the second one is more round.
This agent...
These are here to replace slash enhance humans in places where things need to be kept very secure.
So internal data, internal information.
If you're building something for an HR team, there is a lot of very, very sensitive data.
And open source is by default the most open way of understanding what something is doing.
And also open source is something that you can deploy and have full control over.
